The next Three questions pertain to the following Trader 1 is endowed with 100 identical
Left shoes. Trader 2 is endowed with 99 identical Right shoes. Each trader’s utility from
her allocation of shoes is equal to the mumber of complete pairs of shoes in the allocation.
Traders 1 and 2 trade shoes in competitive markets and arrive at a competitive equilibrium.
Assume that shoes are infinitely divisible.

second die. Which of the following is true?
options: (A) X −Y and X +Y are dependent random variables
(B) X2 and Y are independent random variable
(C) X2 and Y2 are independent random variable
